title: Plugin Manifest
description: Schema for workflow engine plugin manifests in the GoCodeAlone/workflow-registry

name.description: Unique plugin name (e.g. http, messaging, my-custom-plugin)

description.description: Short description of what this plugin provides

source.description: Source repository (Go module path or URL)

path.description: Path within the source repository to the plugin package

type.description: Plugin type: builtin (shipped with engine), external (community/third-party), ui (frontend extension)

tier.description: Plugin tier: core (maintained by GoCodeAlone), community (third-party), premium (paid)

status.description: Active-usage verification status. 'verified' = pinned in a merged main-branch wfctl.yaml of an active GoCodeAlone project; 'experimental' = compiles + unit-tests pass but no verified production usage; 'deprecated' = scheduled removal.

configProvider.description: Whether the plugin implements the engine's ConfigProvider interface (provides config fragments via the plugin SDK). Mirrors RegistryCapabilities.ConfigProvider in workflow/cmd/wfctl/plugin_registry.go.

moduleTypes.description: Module type identifiers this plugin registers (e.g. http.server, messaging.broker)

stepTypes.description: Pipeline step type identifiers this plugin registers (e.g. step.validate, step.http_call)

triggerTypes.description: Trigger type identifiers this plugin registers (e.g. http, schedule, event)

workflowHandlers.description: Workflow handler type identifiers this plugin registers (e.g. http, pipeline, statemachine)

wiringHooks.description: Post-init wiring hook names this plugin provides

serviceMethods.description: gRPC service methods this plugin exposes via the engine (e.g. audit.collector/query). Mirrors RegistryCapabilities.ServiceMethods in workflow/cmd/wfctl/plugin_registry.go.

iacStateBackends.description: IaC state-storage backends this plugin's drivers can manage (e.g. spaces, gcs, s3). Provider-specific list — not all IaC providers expose a state backend, so this is separate from iacProvider.resourceTypes.

resourceTypes.description: Top-level resource types this plugin can manage when it does not publish a nested iacProvider block.

iacProvider.description: IaC provider declaration — name and supported resource types

name.description: Canonical provider name used for discovery (e.g. "digitalocean", "aws", "gcp")

resourceTypes.description: Resource types this provider's drivers can manage (e.g. infra.container_service, infra.k8s_cluster)

supportedCanonicalKeys.description: Canonical config keys this provider's drivers can map (e.g. name, region, image, env_vars)

configSchema.description: Provider-specific schema for resource configuration fragments, keyed by resource type (e.g. infra.container_service → { field: { type, enum, default, description } }). Free-form because each provider's per-resource schema is independent.

migrationDrivers.description: Migration driver names this plugin provides (e.g. golang-migrate, goose)

cliCommands.description: Dynamic wfctl CLI subcommand registrations (workflow v0.18.x+)

name.description: Top-level command name registered with wfctl

flagsPassthrough.description: When true, wfctl passes all flags through to the plugin binary

flags_passthrough.description: Legacy spelling for flagsPassthrough emitted by older plugin manifests

priority.description: Execution priority; lower numbers run first

description.description: Human-readable description of what this hook does

iacProvider.description: IaC provider runtime metadata used by wfctl after plugin install

name.description: Canonical provider name when declared at top level

resourceTypes.description: Resource types this provider's drivers can manage

computePlanVersion.description: wfctl IaC compute/apply plan dispatch version; v2 enables the modern typed provider path

checksums.description: SHA-256 checksums keyed by version string for integrity verification

private.description: Whether this plugin is proprietary / not publicly available

downloads.description: Binary download artifacts for this plugin version

url.description: Download URL for the binary archive

assets.description: Asset capabilities provided by this plugin

config.description: Plugin provides config fragments via ConfigProvider

category.description: Coarse-grained category for UI grouping (e.g. the gocodealone-website plugins page). Optional — manifests without a category render under 'other'.

dependencies.description: Other plugins that must be installed before this plugin can function

name.description: Plugin name as it appears in the registry

required_secrets.description: Secrets the plugin requires to be set in the host environment. Consumed by `wfctl secrets setup --plugin <name>` to drive interactive prompts.

sensitive.description: When true, wfctl prompts via term.ReadPassword (masked) instead of echo

description.description: Human-readable description shown above the prompt

prompt.description: Optional short prompt label (defaults to name)

required_config.description: Non-secret configuration variables the plugin requires. Consumed by `wfctl env setup`/`wfctl vars setup` to drive provider-backed variable prompts.

key.description: Canonical plugin config key used when a manifest maps this value to a custom stored name

sensitive.description: Must be false for required_config; sensitive values belong in required_secrets

description.description: Human-readable description shown above the prompt

prompt.description: Optional short prompt label (defaults to name)

secret_targets.description: Secret provider targets this plugin supports. Consumed by wfctl to present provider-owned scopes such as GitHub repo/org/env, GitLab project/group/env, Vault mount, or local file-backed stores.

provider.description: Canonical secret provider name (e.g. github, gitlab, vault, file, keychain, env)

scopes.description: Provider-owned scope identifiers supported by this target

description.description: Human-readable target description shown by wfctl

config_targets.description: Variable/config provider targets this plugin supports. Consumed by wfctl to present provider-owned scopes such as GitHub repo/org/env variables, GitLab project/group variables, or local file-backed stores.

provider.description: Canonical variable/config provider name (e.g. github, gitlab, env)

scopes.description: Provider-owned scope identifiers supported by this target

description.description: Human-readable target description shown by wfctl
